name: node-readable-stream
title: node-readable-stream
summary: stream compatibility library for Node.js and browser
description:
stream compatibility library for Node.js and browser
node-readable-stream package is a port to browser context of the well
known stream API of Node.js, usable both under Node.js or inside a browser.
.
A stream is an abstract interface for working with streaming data in Node.js.
There are many stream objects provided by Node.js. For instance, a request
to an HTTP server and process.stdout are both stream instances.
.
Node.js is an event-based server-side JavaScript engine.
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use node-readable-stream because stream compatibility library for Node.js and browser.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use node-readable-stream as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair node-readable-stream with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `node-readable-stream --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
